designations.designation: SSS Pain Interference - Pain intensity numeric rating
In the past 7 days, how would you rate your current pain level

definitions.definition: Self-reported pain severity on a zero-to-ten numeric scale. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Pain Interference - Pain location body region
Over the past 2 weeks, primary body area where pain is experienced

definitions.definition: Anatomical region identified as the principal site of pain. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Pain Interference - Pain episode frequency
During the past 4 weeks, how often pain episodes occur

definitions.definition: Count of distinct pain episodes within the assessment period. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Pain Interference - Pain onset timing
In the past 7 days, when did the current pain episode begin

definitions.definition: Date or interval since the onset of the present pain complaint. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Pain Interference - Pain duration per episode
Over the past 2 weeks, how long each pain episode typically lasts

definitions.definition: Average duration of a single pain episode in hours or minutes. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Pain Interference - Overall symptom severity rating
During the past 4 weeks, global rating of symptom burden experienced by the subject

definitions.definition: Composite severity score across all reported symptoms. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: FAB Physical Function - Grip strength measurement
In the past 7 days, maximum force exerted by the hand during a grip test

definitions.definition: Peak isometric grip force measured with a hand dynamometer. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Physical Function - Walking distance achieved
Over the past 2 weeks, maximum distance walked during a timed test

definitions.definition: Total distance covered in a standardized timed walking assessment. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Physical Function - Stair climbing ability
During the past 4 weeks, number of stairs ascended without assistance

definitions.definition: Count of steps climbed independently during functional testing. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Physical Function - Lifting capacity assessment
In the past 7 days, maximum weight safely lifted from floor to waist height

definitions.definition: Heaviest load successfully lifted during a standardized lift test. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Physical Function - Functional impairment score
Over the past 2 weeks, degree of limitation in daily physical activities

definitions.definition: Rating of the subject's functional limitation on a standardized scale. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Physical Function - Joint range of motion
During the past 4 weeks, angular excursion of the affected joint

definitions.definition: Goniometric measurement of active range of motion in degrees. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: Analgesic medication usage
Type and dose of pain medication currently taken

definitions.definition: Record of analgesic agents administered and their dosage. A field of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: Pain catastrophizing score
Tendency to magnify or ruminate on pain sensations

definitions.definition: Score on a questionnaire measuring negative cognitive responses to pain. A field of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: Pain trigger identification
Activities or conditions that provoke pain episodes

definitions.definition: Classification of stimuli that reliably elicit or worsen pain. A field of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: Age at assessment
In the past 7 days, subject's age at the time of the clinical evaluation

definitions.definition: Chronological age of the participant at the assessment date, assessed in the past 7 days.

designations.designation: Comorbidity count
Over the past 2 weeks, number of concurrent medical conditions reported

definitions.definition: Total number of diagnosed comorbid conditions at the time of assessment, assessed over the past 2 weeks.

designations.designation: Quality of life impact rating
Effect of pain on overall quality of life

definitions.definition: Self-rated impact of the condition on daily well-being.

designations.designation: Treatment response classification
Category of response to the current treatment regimen

definitions.definition: Classification of the subject's response to prescribed treatment.

designations.designation: Referred pain distribution
Pattern of pain radiation from the primary site

definitions.definition: Body regions to which pain is referred from the primary source.

designations.designation: SSS Cognitive Difficulty - Working memory span score
In the past 7 days, number of items correctly recalled in sequence

definitions.definition: The maximum number of stimulus items the subject can maintain and reproduce in correct order during a standardized working memory task. Working memory capacity predicts performance on complex cognitive operations and declines with age and neurological impairment. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Cognitive Difficulty - Sustained attention duration
Over the past 2 weeks, length of time the subject maintains focused attention on the task

definitions.definition: The continuous period during which the subject correctly detects target stimuli in a vigilance task, measured in minutes. Sustained attention is a prerequisite for higher-order cognitive processing and is sensitive to fatigue and medication effects. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Cognitive Difficulty - Processing speed index
During the past 4 weeks, rate at which simple cognitive operations are completed

definitions.definition: The number of correct responses per unit time on a symbol-coding or pattern-matching task. Processing speed reflects the efficiency of neural transmission and is one of the earliest cognitive domains to show age-related decline. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Cognitive Difficulty - Verbal fluency word count
In the past 7 days, number of words generated within a time-limited category task

definitions.definition: The total count of unique words produced by the subject within sixty seconds for a given phonemic or semantic category. Verbal fluency draws on lexical retrieval, executive search strategies, and language production networks. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Cognitive Difficulty - Executive function composite rating
Over the past 2 weeks, overall score on tests of planning, inhibition, and cognitive flexibility

definitions.definition: A composite score derived from multiple executive function subtests including trail-making, card sorting, and inhibition tasks. The composite reflects the integrity of prefrontal cortical networks that coordinate goal-directed behavior. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Cognitive Difficulty - Cognitive symptom severity level
During the past 4 weeks, self-reported severity of cognitive complaints

definitions.definition: The subject's rating of the overall severity of cognitive difficulties experienced during the assessment period, recorded on a standardized ordinal scale. Self-reported severity complements objective test scores and captures the subjective burden of cognitive symptoms. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: FAB Daily Living Activities - Task completion time
In the past 7 days, duration required to complete a standardized functional task

definitions.definition: The elapsed time from initiation to successful completion of a defined daily-living task, measured in seconds. Prolonged task completion times indicate motor or cognitive impairment that interferes with independent functioning. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Daily Living Activities - Instrumental activities score
Over the past 2 weeks, performance rating on complex daily living activities

definitions.definition: A composite score reflecting the subject's ability to perform instrumental activities of daily living such as managing finances, preparing meals, using transportation, and managing medications. Lower scores indicate greater dependence on caregiver assistance. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Daily Living Activities - Social participation frequency
During the past 4 weeks, number of social activities engaged in during the assessment period

definitions.definition: The count of social interactions and community activities in which the subject participates, recorded over a defined time frame. Reduced social participation is associated with cognitive decline and depressive symptoms. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Daily Living Activities - Communication effectiveness rating
In the past 7 days, ability to convey and comprehend information in conversation

definitions.definition: A clinician-rated assessment of the subject's functional communication ability, including speech production, comprehension, and pragmatic language use. Communication effectiveness influences social participation and treatment adherence. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Daily Living Activities - Daily functional independence level
Over the past 2 weeks, degree of independence in performing routine daily tasks

definitions.definition: A composite rating of the subject's ability to independently perform basic and instrumental activities of daily living without assistance. Functional independence is the primary outcome measure for rehabilitation interventions and long-term care planning. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Daily Living Activities - Problem solving accuracy
During the past 4 weeks, proportion of novel problems correctly solved

definitions.definition: The percentage of standardized novel reasoning problems that the subject solves correctly within the allotted time. Problem solving accuracy reflects fluid intelligence and the capacity to adapt to unfamiliar situations. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: Cognitive medication usage
Pharmacological agents prescribed for cognitive symptoms

definitions.definition: Record of medications prescribed to manage cognitive symptoms, including cholinesterase inhibitors, memantine, and stimulant agents. Medication type and dosage are documented to assess potential effects on cognitive test performance. A field of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: Cognitive decline trajectory
Rate and pattern of change in cognitive test scores over time

definitions.definition: The longitudinal trend in cognitive assessment scores across multiple evaluation time points, characterized as stable, gradual decline, or accelerated decline. Trajectory classification informs prognosis and treatment decisions. A field of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: Neuropsychological battery composite
Aggregated score across a standardized battery of cognitive tests

definitions.definition: A single composite score derived by combining age-adjusted standard scores from multiple cognitive domains including memory, attention, language, visuospatial, and executive function subtests. The composite provides a global index of cognitive ability. A field of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: Participant age at evaluation
In the past 7 days, age of the participant at the time of cognitive testing

definitions.definition: The chronological age of the research participant at the date of the cognitive evaluation session. Age is the strongest demographic predictor of cognitive performance and is required for computing age-adjusted normative scores, assessed in the past 7 days.

designations.designation: Concurrent medical condition count
Over the past 2 weeks, number of active medical diagnoses at the time of evaluation

definitions.definition: The total number of active comorbid medical conditions documented in the participant's medical record at the evaluation date. Comorbidity burden affects cognitive performance through vascular, metabolic, and pharmacological pathways, assessed over the past 2 weeks.

designations.designation: Cognitive quality of life impact
Effect of cognitive difficulties on daily quality of life

definitions.definition: The self-reported impact of cognitive limitations on the participant's overall quality of life, including social relationships, occupational functioning, and personal autonomy. Quality of life impact may not correlate with objective test scores due to individual differences in coping and compensation.

designations.designation: Cognitive treatment response rating
Clinical assessment of response to cognitive intervention

definitions.definition: The clinician's global impression of the participant's response to cognitive rehabilitation or pharmacological intervention, rated on a standardized scale from marked improvement to marked deterioration. Treatment response guides decisions about continuation, modification, or cessation of therapy.

designations.designation: Digit span forward and backward
Number of digits correctly recalled in forward and reverse order

definitions.definition: The maximum sequence length of digits the subject reproduces correctly in both forward and backward conditions. Forward span assesses auditory attention and short-term memory, while backward span additionally engages working memory and mental manipulation.

designations.designation: SSS Sleep Disturbance - Sleep onset latency
In the past 7 days, time elapsed from lights-out to the onset of sleep

definitions.definition: The duration in minutes from the time the subject attempts to initiate sleep to the first epoch of polysomnographically defined sleep or the subjectively reported moment of falling asleep. Prolonged sleep onset latency is a cardinal feature of insomnia disorder and may reflect heightened cognitive or physiological arousal at bedtime. Latency greater than thirty minutes on most nights is considered clinically significant. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Sleep Disturbance - Sleep efficiency percentage
Over the past 2 weeks, proportion of time in bed actually spent sleeping

definitions.definition: The ratio of total sleep time to total time spent in bed, expressed as a percentage. Sleep efficiency is calculated from sleep diary entries or actigraphy recordings and is a primary outcome in cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia. Efficiency below eighty-five percent is considered indicative of sleep disturbance. Factors that reduce efficiency include prolonged latency, frequent awakenings, and early morning arousal. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Sleep Disturbance - Total sleep time
During the past 4 weeks, cumulative duration of sleep obtained during the sleep period

definitions.definition: The total number of minutes of sleep recorded between sleep onset and final morning awakening, excluding periods of wakefulness. Total sleep time is derived from polysomnography, actigraphy, or sleep diary and is the most commonly reported sleep duration metric. Both short sleep duration of less than six hours and long sleep duration exceeding nine hours are associated with adverse health outcomes including cardiovascular disease and mortality. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Sleep Disturbance - Number of nocturnal awakenings
In the past 7 days, count of discrete awakenings occurring during the sleep period

definitions.definition: The number of times the subject transitions from sleep to wakefulness during the nocturnal sleep period, as determined by polysomnography or self-report. Frequent awakenings fragment sleep architecture, reduce the proportion of restorative slow-wave and rapid-eye-movement sleep, and contribute to daytime fatigue and cognitive impairment. Awakening frequency is elevated in insomnia, sleep apnea, and periodic limb movement disorder. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Sleep Disturbance - Sleep stage distribution
Over the past 2 weeks, percentage of total sleep time spent in each sleep stage

definitions.definition: The proportion of total sleep time occupied by each of the recognized sleep stages: non-rapid-eye-movement stages one, two, and three, and rapid-eye-movement sleep. Stage distribution is determined by visual or automated scoring of polysomnographic recordings according to standardized criteria. Alterations in sleep architecture, such as reduced slow-wave sleep or increased stage-one sleep, may indicate neurodegenerative disease, medication effects, or chronic sleep disruption. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: SSS Sleep Disturbance - Sleep disturbance severity rating
During the past 4 weeks, overall severity of the subject's sleep complaints

definitions.definition: A global rating of the severity of sleep disturbance experienced by the subject, based on standardized self-report or clinician assessment. Severity encompasses difficulty initiating sleep, maintaining sleep, and achieving restorative sleep. The rating captures both the frequency and the impact of sleep complaints on daytime functioning and subjective well-being. Higher severity ratings are associated with greater functional impairment and increased utilization of healthcare resources. As part of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: FAB Emotional Well-Being - Daytime sleepiness score
In the past 7 days, propensity to fall asleep during normal waking activities

definitions.definition: A self-reported measure of the subject's tendency to doze or fall asleep during routine daytime situations such as reading, watching television, or sitting quietly. Excessive daytime sleepiness is quantified using standardized questionnaires that assess sleepiness across multiple situational contexts. Elevated scores indicate insufficient sleep, poor sleep quality, or underlying sleep disorders such as obstructive sleep apnea or narcolepsy. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Emotional Well-Being - Fatigue impact level
Over the past 2 weeks, degree to which fatigue interferes with daily activities

definitions.definition: A self-reported rating of the extent to which persistent fatigue limits the subject's physical, cognitive, and social functioning. Fatigue impact is assessed using validated multi-dimensional scales that distinguish between physical exhaustion, cognitive weariness, and motivational depletion. High fatigue impact scores are common in sleep disorders, depression, and chronic medical conditions and predict reduced quality of life. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Emotional Well-Being - Napping frequency and duration
During the past 4 weeks, number and length of daytime sleep episodes during the recall period

definitions.definition: The count and total duration of intentional or unintentional daytime sleep episodes, reported as the number of naps and average nap duration in minutes. Napping may be compensatory for insufficient nocturnal sleep or may reflect excessive daytime sleepiness from an underlying sleep disorder. Frequent long naps can disrupt the circadian sleep-wake cycle and delay nocturnal sleep onset. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Emotional Well-Being - Bedtime regularity index
In the past 7 days, consistency of the subject's habitual bedtime across nights

definitions.definition: A measure of the variability in the subject's self-selected bedtime over the assessment period, expressed as the standard deviation of bedtime in minutes. Greater bedtime irregularity is associated with circadian misalignment, reduced sleep efficiency, and poorer academic and occupational performance. Regularity is a modifiable behavioral target in sleep hygiene interventions. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Emotional Well-Being - Sleep-related functional impairment
Over the past 2 weeks, impact of poor sleep on the subject's daily functioning

definitions.definition: A composite measure of the degree to which sleep disturbance impairs the subject's performance in occupational, social, and domestic domains. Functional impairment is assessed through self-report questionnaires and clinician observation. Sleep-related impairment mediates the relationship between sleep disturbance and quality of life and is a key outcome in sleep medicine clinical trials. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: FAB Emotional Well-Being - Sleep environment adequacy rating
During the past 4 weeks, suitability of the bedroom environment for restorative sleep

definitions.definition: An assessment of the physical attributes of the sleep environment, including ambient noise level, light exposure, room temperature, mattress comfort, and bed partner disturbance. Suboptimal sleep environment is a common modifiable contributor to sleep onset difficulty and sleep fragmentation. Environmental assessment guides targeted recommendations in behavioral sleep medicine. Based on the Functional Assessment Battery (FAB).

designations.designation: Sleep medication usage record
Prescription and over-the-counter sleep aids currently used

definitions.definition: A record of all pharmacological agents used by the subject to facilitate sleep, including prescription hypnotics, over-the-counter antihistamines, melatonin supplements, and herbal preparations. Medication type, dose, frequency, and duration of use are documented. Chronic use of certain hypnotic agents is associated with tolerance, dependence, and rebound insomnia upon discontinuation. A field of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: Polysomnography apnea-hypopnea index
Number of apnea and hypopnea events per hour of sleep

definitions.definition: The frequency of obstructive, central, and mixed apneas and hypopneas recorded per hour of sleep during overnight polysomnography. The apnea-hypopnea index is the primary diagnostic metric for sleep-disordered breathing. Values above five events per hour indicate mild sleep apnea, above fifteen indicate moderate, and above thirty indicate severe obstructive sleep apnea requiring treatment. A field of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: Circadian rhythm phase marker
Timing of the endogenous circadian rhythm relative to clock time

definitions.definition: The clock time of a physiological marker of the endogenous circadian pacemaker, such as the dim light melatonin onset or the core body temperature nadir. Phase markers indicate whether the individual's biological clock is advanced, delayed, or aligned with the desired sleep-wake schedule. Circadian misalignment is a feature of shift work disorder, jet lag, and delayed or advanced sleep-wake phase disorders. A field of the Symptom Severity Scale (SSS).

designations.designation: Subject age at sleep assessment
In the past 7 days, age of the participant at the time of the sleep evaluation

definitions.definition: The chronological age of the participant at the date of the sleep assessment. Age influences sleep architecture, with progressive reductions in slow-wave sleep and increases in nocturnal wakefulness across the lifespan. Age-adjusted normative data are required for interpreting sleep study results, particularly the apnea-hypopnea index and sleep efficiency thresholds, assessed in the past 7 days.

designations.designation: Sleep-relevant comorbidity count
Over the past 2 weeks, number of medical conditions known to affect sleep quality

definitions.definition: The count of active comorbid conditions that have established associations with sleep disturbance, including chronic pain, depression, anxiety, heart failure, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, and gastroesophageal reflux. The presence and number of sleep-affecting comorbidities guide the differential diagnosis of sleep complaints and influence treatment selection, assessed over the past 2 weeks.

designations.designation: Sleep-related quality of life
Impact of sleep quality on the subject's overall quality of life

definitions.definition: A self-reported measure of the degree to which sleep disturbance affects the subject's perceived quality of life across physical, emotional, and social domains. Sleep-related quality of life captures the subjective consequences of poor sleep that may not be evident from objective sleep metrics alone. Higher scores indicate greater negative impact on well-being and daily satisfaction.

designations.designation: Sleep intervention response rating
Assessment of the subject's response to sleep treatment

definitions.definition: The clinician's global assessment of the subject's response to the prescribed sleep intervention, whether pharmacological, behavioral, or device-based. Response is rated on a standardized scale and considers changes in sleep onset latency, sleep efficiency, total sleep time, and daytime functioning. Responder classification informs decisions about treatment continuation, augmentation, or switching.

designations.designation: Restless leg symptom severity
Intensity and frequency of restless leg syndrome symptoms

definitions.definition: A severity rating for the sensory and motor symptoms of restless leg syndrome, including the urge to move the legs, uncomfortable sensations in the lower extremities, symptom worsening at rest and in the evening, and relief with movement. Severity is assessed using validated rating scales and guides decisions about pharmacological treatment with dopaminergic agents or alpha-2-delta ligands.
